title: Detection of anti-phytopathogenic fungal activity
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5iper","text":"Here we describe the detection of in vitro anti-phytopathogenic fungal activity using the broth dilution method to find the MIC (minimum inhibitory concentration) of an aqueous extract of leaves. Four concentrations were assayed: 1, 2.5, 5, and 10% (v/v) in PDB medium. Each concentration was inoculated with a spore suspension of the selected fungus. PDB without spores was used as negative control and with the addition of a commercial fungicide like positive control. After the inoculation of the fungus, the tubes were incubated for three days in the dark at 26-28 °C. After the incubation time, the tubes were checked for growth of the fungus, if no growth is observed, 25 μL of the suspension are transferred to PDA medium in Petri dishes of 3 cm diameter. Evaluations were made on day 7. The MIC was determined as the lowest concentration of aqueous extract preventing the growth of macroscopically visible mycelium.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9cet2","text":"- 35 x 10 mm polystyrene Petri dish from Corning","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"129k","text":"- Commercial fungicide (Miconazole nitrate, 20 mg/L)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"clbmp","text":"- Culture room for the fungal growth (26 + 2 °C at 12/12 h photoperiod) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2fjmv","text":"- Microscope Axioplan from Zeiss","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"emcrd","text":"- Everything must be done in a laminar flow hood","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"tljo","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ihc","text":"- Petri dishes with PDA medium","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2k42f","text":"- PDB medium","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ebdd1","text":"- Everything should be sterilized","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Performs three dilutions from the 10 % aqueous extract (AE) %s %s CS- conidial suspension (200 conidium/μL) Do not add de CS, only make the mix of AE and water, CS is added tube by tube

Add to a sterile 1.5 mL eppendorf tube mix 1 and then 12.5 μL of CS (in duplicate) Repeat num 1 for the next concentratión and so on.. For the 10% AE just put in each tube 125 μL 10% AE plus 12.5 μL of CS, (in duplicate) The positive control is prepared by adding 1 μL of a commercial fungicide to 125 μL of water and 12.5 μL of CS (in duplicate) Two negative controls: one is only 125 μL of water and the other is 125 μL of PDB and both with 12.5 μL of CS (in duplicate)
Once the conidial suspension has been added, homogenize very well, seal the tubes and leave them in the dark at a temperature of 26-28 ° C for 3 days.
After three days, 25 μL of the suspension is transferred in the center of the PDA medium (polystyrene dish, Corning), in duplicate. The suspension of each eppendorf tube must be perfectly homogenized

Petri dishes are incubated at 26 ± 2 °C at 12/12 photoperiod for 7 days (two replicates /concentration) Evaluation are made on day 7 If the aqueous extract of leaves is fungicidal there should be no growth on the PDA medium, if the extract is not fungicidal then there must be growth of the fungus on the PDA medium
creator: Leticia LPE Peraza Echeverría
